script_system_append: Cast the rider as a heavy-set or plus-size American senior man age 60+ with white or silver hair, paired with the same adult female spouse across connected shots. Follow the emotional direction of Jean's story only at a universal level: quiet freedom near home, a loving spouse nearby, and the feeling of getting back outdoors. Do not recreate any real customer, exact body shape, exact home, exact woods layout, exact ramp build, or testimonial framing.
script_user_append: Keep every ad in seamless real-life B-roll with simple high-success actions only: calm driving, steady seated posture, spouse walking beside or just behind, direct smile, or a small thumbs-up. Leave clean negative space for post-added floating UI overlays such as $599, 500W dual motors, 22-inch seat, 330 LBS, FDA 510(k), 14-Day FREE Return Shipping, and PayPal financing. Do not generate any baked-in captions, signage text, floating words, or logo text inside the footage itself.
scene_pic_system_append: Use the same wheelchair and the same couple in every connected scene. Match the visual proportion cues from c1 and c2: medium or medium-wide portrait framing, most of the seated body and most of the wheelchair visible, minimal empty headroom, and a substantial wide seat that visibly supports a plus-size senior naturally. The rider should visibly fill the seat without dwarfing the chair. Do not let the wheelchair read tiny under the rider, and do not let the rider read shrunken inside an oversized chair.
scene_pic_user_append: Keep the chair in normal open riding position only. The rider should read as a heavy-set or plus-size American senior man 60+ with a broad torso, fuller thighs, and a calm confident expression. His spouse should be an adult woman of similar age range, warm and supportive but never physically assisting unless clearly required. The seat should look wide and supportive, with the rider occupying it naturally like c1 and c2. Use front-side or joystick-side angles only. No folded-chair action, no hand-folding action, no extreme wheel close-up, and no awkward anatomy. During self-operated motion, the right hand must visibly rest on the joystick.
video_prompt_append: Preserve c1 and c2 proportion logic in motion: the rider and wheelchair must move as one believable full-size unit. Keep the rider heavy-set and well-supported by the chair, with the 22-inch seat reading broad and stable, not cartoonishly huge or tiny. Frame the subject like a Meta ad: medium or medium-wide 9:16 lifestyle composition, realistic background, simple successful action, clean negative space for UI overlays, and a strong sales finish without turning the video into a product packshot. The generated footage itself must contain no readable letters, no built-in subtitles, no fake UI text, and no gibberish text artifacts.
